pattern: Height-balanced means every node's two subtree heights differ by at most one — a single bottom-up pass can return a height and flag imbalance at the same time.
hints: The balance condition must hold at every node, not just the root.
If you compute each node's height, balance at a node is just |leftHeight - rightHeight| <= 1.
Compute heights bottom-up and short-circuit: let a subtree report a sentinel once it discovers it is unbalanced.

edge_inputs.description: left subtree is two deeper than the right

stress.description: random level-order tree with holes; mix of balanced and skewed shapes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    arr = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000)]
    # bias toward sparser trees so skewed/unbalanced cases appear
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        arr.append(rng.randint(-10000, 10000) if rng.random() < 0.6 else None)
    return [arr]
stress.note: first element non-null so the root exists
